data.title: S&P Slashes Tether’s USDT Stability Rating to “Weak” Amid Transparency Concerns
data.description:
S&P Global Ratings has downgraded Tether’s USDT to its lowest stability tier, warning that the world’s largest stablecoin is now more vulnerable to market shocks than previously understood.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/sp-slashes-tethers-usdt-stability-rating-to-weak-amid-transparency-concerns/

data.title: Vitalik Buterin: Ethereum’s Growth Will Be “Less Uniform” as Gas Costs Face Targeted Adjustments
data.description:
Just a year after developers — led in part by Vitalik Buterin’s push for scaling — began advocating for higher gas limits, Ethereum is now operating with a 60 million block gas limit, doubling network capacity in only 12 months.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/vitalik-buterin-ethereums-growth-will-be-less-uniform-as-gas-costs-face-targeted-adjustments/

data.title: AI-Driven Growth May Impact U.S. Interest Rate Cuts in 2024
data.description: According to Odaily, the growth driven by artificial intelligence could maintain the strong momentum of the U.S. economy, potentially affecting the Federal Reserve's anticipated interest rate cuts next year. While the market expects the Federal Reserve to implement up to three rate cuts, Dustin Reid from McKinsey & Company suggests that faster growth due to AI might necessitate tighter policies, leading to higher U.S. Treasury yields. He forecasts that by mid-2026, the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury bond will rise from the current 4% to 4.4%.

data.title: Bitcoin's Volatility Decline Signals Institutional Influence
data.description: According to Odaily, Bitcoin's recent decline highlights a fundamental shift in its market dynamics. The cryptocurrency, which has dropped up to 36% since reaching an all-time high in early October, is experiencing reduced implied volatility. This change indicates that institutional involvement is reshaping how risk is transmitted in the Bitcoin market. Previously, Bitcoin's value was largely driven by speculation, with traders seeking to profit from its frequent and significant price swings.

data.title: McKinsey: Artificial intelligence spending may limit the Fed's rate cuts in 2026
data.description: Artificial intelligence-driven growth could keep the US economy strong, thus limiting the extent of the Federal Reserve's expected rate cuts next year. While the market anticipates up to three Fed rate cuts, Dustin Reed of McKinsey & Company says that faster growth driven by AI may require tighter policies, leading to higher US Treasury yields. He predicts that by mid-2026, the yield on the 10-year US Treasury bond will rise from the current 4% to 4.4%. (Jinshi)
